steps.title: Find the interior angle at C using the exterior angle
steps.content:
The angle marked 110° is an exterior angle at point C. Since angles on a straight line sum to 180°, the interior angle ∠BCA is supplementary to the exterior angle:
$$\angle BCA = 180° - 110° = 70°$$

steps.title: Use the isosceles triangle property
steps.content:
We're told that ∠BAC = ∠BCA. Since ∠BCA = 70°, we have:
$$\angle BAC = 70°$$
This means triangle ABC is isosceles with the two base angles equal.

steps.title: Apply the triangle angle sum
steps.content:
The three angles of triangle ABC must sum to 180°:
$$\angle BAC + \angle BCA + \angle ABC = 180°$$
$$70° + 70° + x = 180°$$
$$x = 40°$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
